reasoning: The community prediction is anchored around 3%. A 50% decline from all-time highs for four of the seven largest US tech stocks requires a massive and specific macroeconomic shock or a major popping of a 'tech/AI bubble' reminiscent of the dot-com crash or 2008 GFC. While TSLA is currently significantly off its ATH and high-beta stocks like NVDA or META could plausibly experience large drawdowns in a severe bear market, MSFT, AAPL, and GOOGL have proven much more resilient over the last decade (not dropping 50% even in the 2022 bear market). Since the criteria require at least four to be down 50% simultaneously, this necessitates an exceptional market collapse by 2026, which historically holds a low single-digit chance of occurring over an 18-month window. Therefore, 0.03 aligns with the baseline risk of an extreme tech sector crash.
